title: Andorhal
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 6. The party reaches Andorhal after Arthas's dismissal and enters before the city understands it is being investigated.
openLocation: Andorhal's gates and market district, with inspection offices, granaries, and the mage quarter beyond.
transport: Mounted arrival on Thalassian horses; on foot once inside the city.
immediateObjective: Enter Andorhal under cover, identify how the plague is being embedded at hub level, and map the human infrastructure carrying it.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Sylvanas's perception only
No Kel'Thuzad naming in this chapter
Keep Barov culpability unresolved inside this chapter
No warm trust language stronger than operational dependence

povKnowsAtOpen: Grain is the delivery vector, and the route logic still tightens around Andorhal
Millhaven was the first village fully claimed; much of the intervening country still hides the same weapon behind ordinary-looking sickness
Jaina's sensitivity lets them detect the plague earlier than local authorities do, but they are still following shipments that have already landed rather than preventing rollout
Barov infrastructure appears in the paperwork, but ownership does not yet equal guilt
The repeated grain-side farewell pattern tied to the old seller / newer grain-man file is still weak, unresolved, and not worth diverting from Andorhal yet
Arthas heard the warning and chose the visible war first
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: How the contamination is introduced inside a functioning hub
What the living body contributes after ingestion that the grain alone cannot explain
How many operatives are embedded in Andorhal
Whether the Barovs are architects, collaborators, or merely hosts for someone else's system
Who designed the agent or sits at the top of the network
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No Kel'Thuzad naming in this chapter
No certainty that the Barovs built the plague network
No suggestion that the old seller / newer grain-man clue is solved
No warm trust language stronger than operational dependence
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Andorhal is a functioning distribution center rather than an already-dead city
The contamination is introduced during hub packaging by sophisticated alchemical and necromantic work
The granary district contains embedded operative infrastructure, not random saboteurs
Corin and Havel are inside the trust boundary and a same-night capture is now viable

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: She can still solve this like a deep field hunt if she gets inside Andorhal and reads it harder than the humans who live there.
pov.surfaceObjective: Turn Andorhal from rumor and road logic into a usable picture of who stores the grain, who moves it, and where the city is weakest.
pov.pressureSource: The city is alive, bureaucratic, and layered. Clerks, guards, mages, and civic rhythms are carrying the plague inside ordinary motion.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Sylvanas keeps looking for the decisive field angle — the weak gate, the loose watch, the flank she can turn — as if sharper scouting can solve the whole thing.
pov.chapterTest: Andorhal only opens when the work splits. Jaina handles the arcane and civic doors Sylvanas cannot force with fieldcraft alone.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep treating Jaina's side as support to Sylvanas's command, or let the mission stand on work Sylvanas cannot personally watch line by line.
pov.endStateShift: By chapter close Sylvanas stops thinking of Jaina as help she brought along and starts treating her read as part of the job itself.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 8 opens with Sylvanas ready to divide labor by competence and trust Jaina to close her half without hand-holding.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Contamination analyst, city reader, and the one who can move through Andorhal's magical and bureaucratic layers without losing the thread.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: Proven useful on the road, but still easier to trust when Sylvanas can see the work happening.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Jaina keeps producing answers Sylvanas cannot reach just by watching the street and the room, and she does it without grandstanding. That kills the old asset framing.
throughPov.interactionRule: Sylvanas can think Jaina's name plainly now. The distance is no longer contempt. It lives in caution, workload, and the city's pressure.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Functional cooperation with growing reliance, still run on Sylvanas's command tempo.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The work truly splits here. Each woman has to trust the other to finish the part she cannot stand over and supervise.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Respect is practical and matter-of-fact now. Do not write it as sentiment.
team.doNotOverstateRule: They are not softened. They are simply harder to separate from the mission.

entries.rule: Still with the field party and alive through this chapter.
Introduced here as a local Kirin Tor contact inside Andorhal.
Introduced here as a local militia contact whose instincts are correct even when his framework is incomplete.
Not present. She remains on the northbound warning line after escorting the survivor girl out in Chapter 3.
Background continuity only. Keep it weak and unresolved.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 8 opens the same night in Andorhal with Corin and Havel inside the trust boundary and the capture operation ready to launch.

factsLockedForChapter8: Andorhal is a functioning distribution center, not just another sick city
The contamination is introduced during hub packaging by sophisticated alchemical / necromantic means
The granary district holds an embedded operative structure with at least eight local assets and a two-tier hierarchy
Corin and Havel are now trusted local allies inside Andorhal
The next move is a same-night capture of a live operative
The old seller / newer grain-man clue remains weak, background, and unresolved
factsNotYetEarned: Proof that the wider rollout is already complete
Any collapse of the partnership into overt warmth

scenes.title: The Infiltration
scenes.sceneFunction: Get the party inside Andorhal under plausible cover and add the two local allies who let the investigation operate in layers instead of brute force.
scenes.arcPressure: Andorhal only opens cleanly if Sylvanas lets the mission use doors Jaina can navigate better than she can force.

enrichment.detail: Canon Andorhal is Lordaeron's breadbasket and a main agricultural distribution point in the Eastweald, built around granaries, loading yards, market traffic, and low practical walls rather than fortress architecture. The gate sequence should therefore feel like entry into busy commercial routine, not entry into a visibly dying city.

beats.goal: Enter Andorhal without advertising that the investigation has reached the city itself
beats.conflict: The city is functioning well enough that officials still think in terms of paperwork, jurisdictions, and routine inspections rather than an active plague conspiracy

outcome.summary: The cover holds and gets them through the gate, but the ordinary civic rhythm makes the scale feel worse rather than safer.

beats.valueShift: external target -> living system carrying hidden contamination
newOnPageInformation: Andorhal is still operating as a market city rather than a ruin
Jaina can navigate inspection protocol and bureaucratic tone well enough to make the cover believable
Sylvanas immediately starts reading guards, exits, choke points, and granary traffic
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How the grain is being treated inside the city
Which officials are merely bureaucrats and which belong to the network
beats.transitionIntent: contact-pull: once inside, they need the local mage who has already been seeing the pattern go nowhere

beats.goal: Find a magical contact who can get Jaina into the right rooms and translate what Andorhal's mages have been missing
beats.conflict: Corin is competent but junior, frightened, and already trained by experience to expect his correct reports to vanish into Dalaran procedure

outcome.summary: Corin becomes an ally and opens institutional doors, but his history proves the bureaucracy has already been absorbing and discarding warning signs.

beats.valueShift: living system carrying hidden contamination -> local ally inside failed institutions
newOnPageInformation: Corin has been tracking grain-distribution irregularities for weeks
His reports disappeared into the same kind of bureaucracy Jaina has always trusted
The magical layer of the city has been producing warnings long before the field party arrived
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Corin has seen the mechanism itself or only the symptoms around it
Whether any part of Dalaran's silence is incompetence rather than compromise
beats.transitionIntent: ground-read: Corin can open mage-side doors, but Sylvanas still needs the city read that starts from the street

beats.goal: Add a ground-level local read on Andorhal's granary district and human routines
beats.conflict: Havel is suspicious of outsiders and has only pattern, not proof: sick workers, new grain routes, and a district full of people who behave like civilians until they do not

outcome.summary: Havel gives them the physical city they need, but his evidence is still instinct and repetition rather than a case anyone above him would honor.

beats.valueShift: local ally inside failed institutions -> the city has two reliable witnesses who were ignored for different reasons
newOnPageInformation: Havel has been seeing sickness cluster around the grain system for months
A new supplier pattern entered the city roughly three months ago
Certain workers in the granary district keep military-grade discipline under civilian cover
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the city knows it is hosting enemy infrastructure or merely living atop it
beats.transitionIntent: split-point: with Corin and Havel in place, the investigation can finally divide along the seam the partnership has been building toward

beats.dilemma: Keep the team together for safety, or split Jaina and Sylvanas so each can work at full competency in the layer only she can read
beats.decision: Jaina takes Corin and the mage-side access to study the contamination; Sylvanas takes Havel and the granary district to map the human structure
beats.valueShift: the city has two reliable witnesses who were ignored for different reasons -> the partnership becomes the investigation's operating system
newOnPageInformation: Jaina can function unsupervised in institutional terrain Sylvanas would only slow down
Sylvanas can read embedded operatives and supply discipline in ways Jaina cannot
Cyndia and Lyriel anchor the physical security layer while the leads split
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the city is already reacting to them
Which half of the investigation will reach the real danger first
beats.transitionIntent: parallel-yield: once the split is accepted, the city starts giving up two different halves of the same answer

scenes.title: The Mechanism Revealed
scenes.sceneFunction: Let Jaina solve the how and Sylvanas solve the who until the halves lock together into one infrastructure picture.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Andorhal has both magical and logistical witnesses
The city is hiding a system, not an accident
scenes.arcPressure: The city yields only in split form: Jaina solves the magical contamination while Sylvanas exposes the human structure it rides inside.

enrichment.detail: The plague's grain layer is applied after harvest at the hub, and canon Cult of the Damned infrastructure includes acolytes, necromancers, and alchemical personnel rather than only robed zealots. That supports a packaging-floor mechanism hidden inside ordinary labor, residue in workspaces, and disciplined workers who read more like covert staff than random saboteurs.
enrichment.source: stories/the-plague-road/plans/plague-mechanism.md; stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md; Warcraft Wiki: Cult of the Damned; Warcraft Wiki: Andorhal

beats.goal: Determine exactly how contaminated grain is being treated inside a working distribution hub
beats.conflict: The mechanism is buried inside routine packaging work, meaning Jaina has to read active alchemical traces without alerting everyone standing around them

outcome.summary: She isolates the mechanism cleanly, but the sophistication tells her the enemy is closer to institutional research than hedge sabotage.

beats.valueShift: the partnership becomes the investigation's operating system -> mechanism resolved, threat escalated
newOnPageInformation: The grain is treated during packaging with a necromantic carrier layer rather than fouled at harvest
Milling, moisture, and baking carry that outer shell deeper into ordinary use instead of cleansing it away
The strongest signature in the grain still looks like only the carrier half of the plague, implying the host body finishes the work later
The work shows institutional-grade alchemy and disciplined magical methodology
Jaina's answer lands cleanly even without Sylvanas standing beside her
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Andorhal is the top of the system or merely a mid-chain hub
How the conditioned host becomes undead after death
beats.transitionIntent: human-map: once Jaina has the mechanism, the missing half is the living structure Sylvanas is reading elsewhere

beats.goal: Identify how many living operatives are embedded in the granary district and how they are structured
beats.conflict: The city is full of workers, porters, guards, and clerks; the enemy survives by looking like one more layer of ordinary labor

outcome.summary: Sylvanas maps the local structure and hidden supply behavior, but the top command node stays out of reach.

beats.valueShift: mechanism resolved, threat escalated -> embedded organization exposed
newOnPageInformation: At least eight operatives are embedded in the district
The cell has a two-tier internal hierarchy rather than random zealots
Off-ledger caches and movement patterns prove the conspiracy has been operating here for months
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who sits above the local hierarchy
Whether the city's official leadership is penetrated or merely blind
beats.transitionIntent: table-turn: the two investigations now need to be put on the same table before the city starts moving against them

beats.dilemma: Treat the two findings as adjacent leads, or admit that each woman solved only half a machine neither could have seen alone
beats.decision: Put the data together immediately and build the next move from the combined picture
beats.valueShift: embedded organization exposed -> city-scale conspiracy seen whole
newOnPageInformation: Andorhal is a functioning distribution center weaponized from the inside
Mage-level contamination work and disciplined human logistics are inseparable parts of one system
Jaina has the carrier-side answer and Sylvanas the living-network answer, but the host-side trigger still has to be earned elsewhere
Corin and Havel can support the operation, but neither can replace one of the two leads
Sylvanas cannot honestly describe Jaina's work as support anymore; without that half, there is no case against the city at all
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the city already suspects them
Whether the network has already completed its wider rollout
beats.transitionIntent: operational-choice: once the machine is visible, the next move becomes a question of tracing material or taking a living witness

scenes.title: The Load-Bearing Partnership
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn the Andorhal breakthrough into an operational plan and make Sylvanas admit, privately, that the partnership is no longer optional.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The mechanism and the local organization are both in hand
scenes.arcPressure: The plan only works if Sylvanas treats Jaina's half of the work as irreplaceable instead of auxiliary.

enrichment.detail: Dalaran's Council culture is consensus-heavy, cautious, and slow even when field correspondents are right. Corin therefore fits as useful Kirin Tor ground machinery while Jaina remains wary of the institution above him; the split-plan beat should read as trust in a competent junior mage and in Jaina's judgment, not as sudden restored faith in the whole system.

beats.goal: Decide the next move before the enemy realizes Andorhal has been breached
beats.conflict: Jaina wants the alchemical trail back to its source; Sylvanas wants a living operative who can name the command structure; either move alone leaves half the gap open

outcome.summary: The disagreement resolves into a joint plan instead of a split plan, but that only works because each woman trusts the other to handle the half she cannot.

beats.valueShift: city-scale conspiracy seen whole -> two-part operational plan
newOnPageInformation: A live capture is possible tonight because the network still feels secure in its own city
Jaina's trace work can validate or break whatever a prisoner says
Cyndia and Lyriel are strong enough on perimeter to let the leads commit fully
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the city has already marked Corin or Havel as leaks
How fast the network can erase itself once it senses contact
beats.transitionIntent: private-recognition: once the plan exists, Sylvanas only has to admit what the plan now depends on

beats.dilemma: Keep thinking of Jaina as an attached resource, or admit that removing her now would collapse the investigation she helped make possible
beats.decision: Admit privately that the investigation requires both of them and act from that truth
beats.valueShift: two-part operational plan -> one shared job
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas no longer frames Jaina as an asset in her own thinking
The mission's real structure is now complementary competence, not custody or convenience
Corin and Havel are valuable, but the core engine remains the same four-woman field unit plus the two leads
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who will break first once the capture starts
What the city will do when it realizes it is being hunted from inside
beats.transitionIntent: chapter-handoff: Chapter 8 opens with the capture operation and the moment the hunters discover the city has been hunting back

normalizations: Expanded markdown beat labels into explicit beatType and scenePhase fields for all 9 beats.
Normalized beat 9's value shift to inherit beat 8's exact end state: two-part operational plan -> one shared job.
Preserved the chapter-close handoff locks for Chapter 8 as structured JSON instead of flattening them into summary text.
warnings: All 6 scene beats resolve as yes-but. The cumulative pressure fits an infiltration chapter, but the prose will need to differentiate gate access, ally recruitment, mechanism discovery, and planning beats sharply to avoid a same-note procedural cadence.
Beats 1-4 lean heavily on access-and-discovery pressure before the mechanism reveal hardens the threat, so the chapter's front half depends more on institutional friction than on hard reversals.
